From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English film director, producer and screenwriter (1875–1973)

| George Pearson | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1875-03-19)19 March 1875  [London](/wiki/London "London"), [England](/wiki/England "England") |
| Died | 6 February 1973(1973-02-06) (aged 97)  [Malvern, Worcestershire](/wiki/Malvern,_Worcestershire "Malvern, Worcestershire"), [England](/wiki/England "England") |
| Occupations | Director, producer, screenwriter |
| Years active | 1914–1937 |

**George Pearson** [OBE](/wiki/OBE "OBE"), (19 March 1875 – 6 February 1973) was a pioneering [English](/wiki/England "England") [film director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director"), [producer](/wiki/Film_producer "Film producer") and [screenwriter](/wiki/Screenwriter "Screenwriter"), mainly in the [silent film](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film") era. He was born in [London](/wiki/London "London").

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_Pearson_(filmmaker)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

The only son of George Pearson, a silk tie cutter, George Pearson's first profession after [Culham College](/wiki/Culham_College "Culham College"), near Oxford (not a college of the [University of Oxford](/wiki/University_of_Oxford "University of Oxford")) was teaching. He excelled in this role, becoming a headmaster by 1902 at the age of 26. His major post was at Staples Rd School [Loughton](/wiki/Loughton "Loughton") [Essex](/wiki/Essex "Essex"), on which there is a [blue plaque](/wiki/Blue_plaque "Blue plaque"). He was originally drawn to film making as an educational aid. In 1913 Pearson entered the film industry, initially as a script writer.

Pearson worked for [Gaumont](/wiki/Gaumont_Film_Company "Gaumont Film Company") and later joined the [Colonial Film Unit](/wiki/Colonial_Film_Unit "Colonial Film Unit") (later the [Commonwealth Film Unit](/wiki/Commonwealth_Film_Unit "Commonwealth Film Unit")), remaining in employment until the age of 81. Though little of his work is known to have survived, Pearson is credited with pioneering the use of the moving camera shot.

## Awards and personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_Pearson_(filmmaker)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Awards and personal life")]

In 1951, he was awarded the [OBE](/wiki/Officer_of_the_Order_of_the_British_Empire "Officer of the Order of the British Empire"). Pearson married Edith Stacey (1881-1961) in 1901. They had two sons and two daughters. One daughter, Winifred, worked as a film editor. He died in [Great Malvern](/wiki/Great_Malvern "Great Malvern"), [Worcestershire](/wiki/Worcestershire "Worcestershire") in 1973, aged nearly 98, outliving Winifred and his elder son, Malcolm, a surgeon. In 1957, his [autobiography](/wiki/Autobiography "Autobiography"), *Flashback*, was published.

## Selected filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_Pearson_(filmmaker)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Selected filmography")]

* *[Peg Woffington](/wiki/Peg_Woffington_(1912_film) "Peg Woffington (1912 film)")* (1912)
* *[The Fool](/wiki/The_Fool_(1913_film) "The Fool (1913 film)")* (1913)
* *[Heroes of the Mine](/wiki/Heroes_of_the_Mine_(1913_film) "Heroes of the Mine (1913 film)")* (1913)
* *[A Study in Scarlet](/wiki/A_Study_in_Scarlet_(1914_British_film) "A Study in Scarlet (1914 British film)")* ([1914](/wiki/1914_in_film "1914 in film"))
* *[John Halifax, Gentleman](/wiki/John_Halifax,_Gentleman_(1915_film) "John Halifax, Gentleman (1915 film)")* (1915)
* *[A Cinema Girl's Romance](/wiki/A_Cinema_Girl%27s_Romance "A Cinema Girl's Romance")* (1915)
* *Ultus and the Grey Lady* ([1916](/wiki/1916_in_film "1916 in film"))
* *[Sally Bishop](/wiki/Sally_Bishop_(1916_film) "Sally Bishop (1916 film)")* (1916)
* *[The Kiddies in the Ruins](/wiki/The_Kiddies_in_the_Ruins "The Kiddies in the Ruins")* (1918)
* *[The Romance of Old Bill](/wiki/The_Romance_of_Old_Bill "The Romance of Old Bill")* (1918)
* *[Nothing Else Matters](/wiki/Nothing_Else_Matters_(film) "Nothing Else Matters (film)")* ([1920](/wiki/1920_in_film "1920 in film"))
* *[Mary Find the Gold](/wiki/Mary_Find_the_Gold "Mary Find the Gold")* (1921)
* *[Squibs Wins the Calcutta Sweep](/wiki/Squibs_Wins_the_Calcutta_Sweep "Squibs Wins the Calcutta Sweep")* ([1922](/wiki/1922_in_film "1922 in film"))
* *[Mord Em'ly](/wiki/Mord_Em%27ly "Mord Em'ly")* (1922)
* *[Squibs' Honeymoon](/wiki/Squibs%27_Honeymoon "Squibs' Honeymoon")* (1923)
* *[Squibs M.P.](/wiki/Squibs_M.P. "Squibs M.P.")* (1923)
* *[Love, Life and Laughter](/wiki/Love,_Life_and_Laughter_(1923_film) "Love, Life and Laughter (1923 film)")* ([1923](/wiki/1923_in_film "1923 in film"))
* *[The Romany](/wiki/The_Romany "The Romany")* (1923)
* *[Reveille](/wiki/Reveille_(film) "Reveille (film)")* ([1924](/wiki/1924_in_film "1924 in film"))
* *[The Little People](/wiki/The_Little_People_(film) "The Little People (film)")* ([1926](/wiki/1926_in_film "1926 in film"))
* *[Blinkeyes](/wiki/Blinkeyes "Blinkeyes")* (1926)
* *[Huntingtower](/wiki/Huntingtower_(film) "Huntingtower (film)")* ([1927](/wiki/1927_in_film "1927 in film"))
* *[Love's Option](/wiki/Love%27s_Option "Love's Option")* (1928)
* *[Auld Lang Syne](/wiki/Auld_Lang_Syne_(1929_film) "Auld Lang Syne (1929 film)")* ([1929](/wiki/1929_in_film "1929 in film"))
* *[Journey's End](/wiki/Journey%27s_End "Journey's End")* ([1930](/wiki/1930_in_film "1930 in film"))
* *[East Lynne on the Western Front](/wiki/East_Lynne_on_the_Western_Front "East Lynne on the Western Front")* (1931)
* *[The Good Companions](/wiki/The_Good_Companions_(1933_film) "The Good Companions (1933 film)")* ([1933](/wiki/1933_in_film "1933 in film")) (associate producer)
* *[A Shot in the Dark](/wiki/A_Shot_in_the_Dark_(1933_film) "A Shot in the Dark (1933 film)")* (1933)
* *[Whispering Tongues](/wiki/Whispering_Tongues "Whispering Tongues")* (1934)
* *[The Ace of Spades](/wiki/The_Ace_of_Spades "The Ace of Spades")* (1935)
* *[That's My Uncle](/wiki/That%27s_My_Uncle "That's My Uncle")* (1935)
* *[Jubilee Window](/wiki/Jubilee_Window "Jubilee Window")* (1935)
* *[Checkmate](/wiki/Checkmate_(1935_film) "Checkmate (1935 film)")* (1935)
* *[Midnight at Madame Tussaud's](/wiki/Midnight_at_Madame_Tussaud%27s "Midnight at Madame Tussaud's")* (1936)
* *[Follow Your Star](/wiki/Follow_Your_Star "Follow Your Star")* (1938)
